mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >65% of months following a rate cut, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >70% of instances where the unemployment rate crosses the threshold, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio < 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and credit availability, which directly boosts corporate revenues and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in >60% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply chain normalization, which triggers short-term momentum buying and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months following industrial production acceleration, or the signal shows zero predictive power during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index growth above 5 percent forces expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which increases discount rates and compresses equity valuations, yielding negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in >65% of months when CPI exceeds the threshold, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms broad-based labor market strength, supporting consumer spending and corporate profit growth for positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal predicts negative 60-day returns in >50% of observations, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly gross domestic product growth reflects aggregate economic expansion, which translates into higher corporate earnings and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >60% of quarters following positive GDP prints, or the signal Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly contraction in industrial production signals immediate supply chain bottlenecks and manufacturing weakness, triggering short-term risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in >60% of months following the production decline threshold, or the signal fails to generate a statistically significant negative alpha.
